source: Efficient and robust approximate nearest neighbor search using Hierarchical Navigable Small World graphs.pdf

concepts.finding: HNSW builds a multi-layer graph structure where upper layers have longer edges (coarse search) and lower layers have shorter edges (fine search), achieving logarithmic complexity and strong recall on billion-scale datasets.
concepts.implication: Hierarchical graph-based ANN search provides state-of-the-art efficiency for embedding-based retrieval in production search systems with controllable recall-speed tradeoff.
concepts.open_question: What is the optimal layer-capping strategy for HNSW when embedding distribution is non-uniform?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: HNSW-based vector retrieval will achieve >95% recall@10 on 1B+ document collections with sub-10ms average query latency.

concepts.finding: Diverse neighbor selection (not just closest neighbors) during graph construction prevents hubs and improves search quality on clustered data.
concepts.implication: Diverse neighbor selection is applicable to building better document similarity graphs for search.
concepts.open_question: How should diversity vs. proximity be balanced in nearest neighbor graph construction for different data distributions?
concepts.testable_hypothesis: A diverse neighbor selection strategy in ANN graph construction will improve recall by 5%+ on highly clustered embedding spaces.
